clinical_coherence.notes: 45M with DM2, chronic sinusitis, and cephalosporin allergy presenting with classic meningitis triad (fever 39.5, nuchal rigidity, AMS). Vitals, exam, PMH, and medications all form a highly plausible clinical picture. The sinusitis as a contiguous source is well-established.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF shows classic bacterial meningitis: WBC 3414 with 94% neutrophils, protein 322.5, glucose 13, Gram-positive diplococci on stain. MRI shows diffuse leptomeningeal enhancement with sulcal DWI restriction and paranasal sinus disease. All concordant with pneumococcal meningitis from sinus source.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G00.1 correct for pneumococcal meningitis. Critical actions appropriately include empiric antibiotics, dexamethasone, blood cultures. Contraindicated actions are clinically sound. Cephalosporin allergy is noted and correctly addressed in management plan.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic presentation with textbook CSF findings, positive Gram stain, full meningitis triad, and high fever. Appropriately labeled straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 45-year-old African American male with DM2 and chronic sinusitis is a realistic profile for community-acquired pneumococcal meningitis. The sinusitis predisposition is well-documented.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case for classic bacterial meningitis with cephalosporin allergy forcing antibiotic modification. The sinusitis source and cephalosporin allergy add clinical complexity without obscuring the diagnosis.

strengths: Cephalosporin allergy adds meaningful management complexity
Sinus source provides clear pathophysiologic coherence
